_🧹 Nitpick_ | _🔵 Trivial_ | _💤 Low value_

**Consider moving exchange links to configuration.**

The hardcoded `EXCHANGE_LINKS` array could be moved to environment configuration or a constants file to allow updates without code changes. However, this is acceptable for an MVP/initial implementation.

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

In `@apps/web/src/app/`[lang]/onboarding/_components/onboarding-adventure-page.tsx
around lines 29 - 33, The EXCHANGE_LINKS constant is hardcoded in
onboarding-adventure-page.tsx; extract it into a configurable source (either
environment-driven or a dedicated constants/config module) and import it into
the component instead of defining it inline: create an exported value (e.g.,
export const EXCHANGE_LINKS) in a config file that preserves the shape/readonly
typing (or reads from env with a fallback default), update
onboarding-adventure-page.tsx to import EXCHANGE_LINKS, and ensure any usages of
EXCHANGE_LINKS in the file continue to work unchanged.
```

</details>

**Expose error state from `useAllSpaces` hook.**

The `useAllSpaces` hook doesn't return an error property from useSWR. When the spaces fetch fails, `spaces` defaults to an empty array and the component displays "no spaces available" — making network errors indistinguishable from genuinely empty spaces. Modify the hook to return `{ spaces, isLoading, error }` so the component can handle and communicate fetch failures appropriately.

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

In `@apps/web/src/app/`[lang]/onboarding/_components/onboarding-adventure-page.tsx
at line 45, The hook useAllSpaces currently returns { spaces, isLoading } but
hides useSWR's error, causing failures to appear as empty results; update
useAllSpaces to capture and return the SWR error (e.g., const { data: spaces,
error, isLoading } = useSWR(...)) and change its export to return { spaces,
isLoading, error } so callers like the onboarding component can detect and
display network/fetch errors instead of treating them as empty arrays.
```

</details>

_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_ | _⚡ Quick win_ **Add accessibility attributes for icons and QR code.** Several accessibility improvements needed: 1. Decorative icons in card titles should have `aria-hidden="true"` (lines 168, 187, 204, 222, 289, 305) 2. The QR code (line 313) needs an accessible label via `aria-label` or a visually-hidden description
♿ Proposed fix ```diff - + {t('explore.title')} {/* Apply similar fix to other decorative icons */} -
+
```
As per coding guidelines, ensure semantic HTML and ARIA attributes for accessibility.
🤖 Prompt for AI Agents ``` Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate. In `@apps/web/src/app/`[lang]/onboarding/_components/onboarding-adventure-page.tsx around lines 168 - 313, Add missing accessibility attributes: mark decorative icon components (Compass, PlusCircle, Handshake, Wallet, Copy, ExternalLink) used in the card titles and buttons as aria-hidden="true" so screen readers ignore them, and give the QRCode in DepositDetailsCard an accessible name (e.g., aria-label="Space deposit address QR code" or supply a visually-hidden description) so it is announced to assistive tech; update the JSX in renderSelectorCard usages and the DepositDetailsCard (specifically the QRCode element) accordingly. ```
✅ Addressed in commits 504dd0d to e094f10

**Remove redundant type assertion.**

Line 286 uses `as string` but `space.address` is already checked on line 263. The type assertion is unnecessary.




```diff
- onClick={() => onCopyAddress(space.address as string)}
+ onClick={() => onCopyAddress(space.address)}
```

_🧹 Nitpick_ | _🔵 Trivial_ | _💤 Low value_

**Consider validating address format before QR generation.**

The QR code is generated directly from `space.address` without validation. If the address is malformed, the QR code will be invalid. Consider adding address format validation or relying on backend validation guarantees.

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

In `@apps/web/src/app/`[lang]/onboarding/_components/onboarding-adventure-page.tsx
at line 313, Validate space.address before passing it to the QRCode component:
implement or use a helper like isValidAddress(address) (or a regex/validator)
and in the onboarding-adventure-page component only render <QRCode
value={space.address} /> when isValidAddress(space.address) is true; otherwise
render a fallback UI (placeholder, error message, or disable the QR generation)
and log/handle the invalid address case so malformed addresses don't produce
invalid QR codes.
```

</details>

**Fix fallback path to include locale prefix.**

The fallback to `/profile` when `lang` is undefined is inconsistent with the app's routing structure. Both the profile route (`/[lang]/profile`) and onboarding route (`/[lang]/onboarding`) require a locale prefix. Update the fallback to `/${lang || 'en'}/profile` to ensure the path is valid regardless of whether `lang` is undefined.

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

In `@apps/web/src/hooks/use-create-profile.ts` around lines 69 - 71, The fallback
path when building onboardingPath uses '/profile' without a locale; update the
logic that computes onboardingPath (using params and lang in useCreateProfile /
the const lang = params?.lang and onboardingPath) to include a locale prefix by
default—e.g., construct the path as `/${lang || 'en'}/profile` when lang is
missing so router.push(onboardingPath) always navigates to a valid
locale-prefixed route.
```

</details>

_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_ | _⚡ Quick win_

**Guard against undefined `lang` in onboarding URL.**

If `lang` is undefined from `useParams()`, the URL becomes `/undefined/onboarding`. Add a fallback to ensure a valid route.




<details>
<summary>🛡️ Proposed fix</summary>

```diff
- onboardingUrl={`/${lang}/onboarding`}
+ onboardingUrl={lang ? `/${lang}/onboarding` : undefined}
```

This ensures the onboarding link won't appear if the lang parameter is missing, preventing broken navigation.
</details>

<!-- suggestion_start -->

```

<!-- suggestion_end -->

```

rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

In `@packages/epics/src/people/components/button-profile.connected.tsx` at line
124, The onboarding URL currently uses lang (from useParams()) directly causing
`/undefined/onboarding` when lang is missing; update the ButtonProfileConnected
render to guard against undefined lang: either conditionally omit rendering the
onboarding link/button when lang is falsy or compute onboardingUrl only when
lang is set (e.g., const onboardingUrl = lang ? `/${lang}/onboarding` :
undefined) and pass it accordingly to the component so no broken
`/undefined/onboarding` route is emitted; look for uses of lang, onboardingUrl
and the ButtonProfileConnected/ButtonProfile component to implement the guard.
```

_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_ | _⚡ Quick win_

**Add an explicit accessible label to the search input.**

The input relies on placeholder text only; screen-reader users may not get a stable control label. Please add an explicit label (`aria-label` at minimum).

 

<details>
<summary>♿ Suggested fix</summary>

```diff
           <Input
             value={query}
             onChange={(event) => onQueryChange(event.target.value)}
             placeholder={t('spaceSearchPlaceholder')}
+            aria-label={title}
             disabled={disabled}
           />
```
</details>

As per coding guidelines, `apps/web/**` requires accessibility support with semantic HTML and ARIA attributes.

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

In `@apps/web/src/app/`[lang]/onboarding/_components/onboarding-adventure-page.tsx
around lines 151 - 156, The search Input currently relies on placeholder text
only; update the Input component used in onboarding-adventure-page.tsx to add an
explicit accessible label (e.g., pass an aria-label or aria-labelledby prop) so
screen readers have a stable control name; modify the Input invocation that uses
value={query}, onChange={(event) => onQueryChange(event.target.value)},
placeholder={t('spaceSearchPlaceholder')}, disabled={disabled} to include
something like aria-label={t('spaceSearchLabel')} (or reference an existing
visible label via aria-labelledby) ensuring the label string key
(spaceSearchLabel) is added to translations if needed.
```

</details>

🌐 Proposed fix ```diff
{evmAddressPattern.test(address) ? ( ) : (

- Invalid treasury address format. + {t('depositDetails.invalidAddress')}

)}
```
As per coding guidelines, `apps/web/**` must use next-intl integration and avoid hardcoded user-facing strings.
🤖 Prompt for AI Agents ``` Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate. In `@apps/web/src/app/`[lang]/onboarding/_components/onboarding-adventure-page.tsx around lines 438 - 448, This UI and accessibility text is hardcoded: the QRCode aria-label and the fallback paragraph string should be localized via next-intl. Import and use the translation hook (e.g., useTranslations) in the component that renders QRCode and the invalid message, replace the literal aria-label template and "Invalid treasury address format." with translated strings (passing space.title into the interpolated translation for the aria-label), and use the same translation keys for the visible paragraph so all user-facing copy uses next-intl instead of hardcoded English; locate references to evmAddressPattern and QRCode to update the surrounding JSX. ```
